relationship.description: Foreign key relationship to workday_employee_skills_development.employee_id - enables linking training assignments to skill development outcomes for the same employee

properties.description: ID of the user or Unique identifier for the employee

relationship.description: Reference relationship to workday_employee_skills_development.manager_name - enables consistent manager-level reporting across training completion and skill development
Foreign key relationship to workday_employee_skills_development.training_curriculum_title - connects training programs to their skill development outcomes

properties.description: Title of the curriculum or training program

relationship.description: Reference relationship to workday_employee_skills_development.division - enables division-level cross-analysis of training completion and skill development metrics

properties.description: Business division name or department name that the employee belongs to

relationship.description: Primary foreign key relationship to csod_training_records.user_id - establishes the core employee connection between training activities and skill development outcomes

properties.description: Employee identifier matching CSOD user_id for cross-reference

relationship.description: Hierarchical relationship to workday_employee_skills_development.employee_id - enables manager-employee hierarchy analysis within skills development
Reference relationship to csod_training_records.manager_name - enables consistent manager-level analysis across training completion and skill development systems

properties.description: Name of the employee's direct manager

relationship.description: Reference relationship to csod_training_records.division - enables cross-system division-level analysis of training effectiveness and skill development outcomes

properties.description: Business division matching CSOD division for alignment

relationship.description: Foreign key relationship to training_skill_mapping.curriculum_id - links skill development to specific training curricula through bridge table

properties.description: ID linking to CSOD curriculum that developed this skill

relationship.description: Foreign key relationship to csod_training_records.curriculum_title - directly connects skill development outcomes to the training programs that produced them

properties.description: Title of training curriculum from CSOD

relationship.description: Foreign key relationship to csod_training_records.curriculum_title - establishes which training programs are mapped to develop specific skills

properties.description: Training curriculum that develops the associated skill

relationship.description: Foreign key relationship to workday_employee_skills_development.skill_name - defines which skills are developed by specific training curricula

properties.description: Skill that is developed by the associated curriculum

relationship_types_explained.one-to-many: One parent record can have multiple child records (e.g., one employee can have many training assignments)
relationship_types_explained.many-to-one: Multiple child records relate to one parent record (e.g., many skill assessments relate to one employee)
relationship_types_explained.many-to-many: Records can have multiple relationships in both directions (implemented via bridge table)
relationship_types_explained.reference: Lookup relationship for dimensional analysis (e.g., division names for grouping)
relationship_types_explained.self-referencing: Table references itself (e.g., manager-employee hierarchy within same table)

employee_to_skills.description: Join employee training completion with skill development outcomes
employee_to_skills.sql: SELECT c.user_id, c.full_name, c.curriculum_title, c.transcript_status, w.skill_name, w.proficiency_improvement FROM csod_training_records c INNER JOIN workday_employee_skills_development w ON c.user_id = w.employee_id AND c.curriculum_title = w.training_curriculum_title

training_effectiveness.description: Analyze training program effectiveness across both completion and skill development
training_effectiveness.sql: SELECT c.curriculum_title, COUNT(c.id) as total_assignments, COUNT(CASE WHEN c.transcript_status = 'Satisfied' THEN 1 END) as completions, AVG(w.proficiency_improvement) as avg_skill_improvement FROM csod_training_records c LEFT JOIN workday_employee_skills_development w ON c.user_id = w.employee_id AND c.curriculum_title = w.training_curriculum_title GROUP BY c.curriculum_title

manager_team_analysis.sql: SELECT c.manager_name, COUNT(DISTINCT c.user_id) as team_members, AVG(CASE WHEN c.transcript_status = 'Satisfied' THEN 1.0 ELSE 0.0 END) as completion_rate, AVG(w.proficiency_improvement) as avg_skill_improvement FROM csod_training_records c LEFT JOIN workday_employee_skills_development w ON c.user_id = w.employee_id AND c.manager_name = w.manager_name GROUP BY c.manager_name
